rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Amended Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Relief From Government Claim Filing Requirements
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, March 20, 2025 Line 1. PETITIONER ASHOD DONIKIAN Second Amended Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Relief From Government Claim Filing Requirements. Petitioner Ashod Donikian's petition for relief from Government Act claims filing requirements is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MINNA MILLARE VS. SPINNAKER INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses Of Defendant Spinnaker Insurance Company
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, March 20, 2025 Line 6. 1 - PLAINTIFF MINNA MILLARE's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses Of Defendant Spinnaker Insurance Company. Plaintiff Minna Millare's motion to compel further discovery responses to her document requests 32 and 33 by defendant Spinnaker Insurance Company is denied. The declaration of Yvette Borrego establishes that compliance by Spinnaker with requests 32 and 33 would be unduly burdensome. Ms. Millare's faulting of Spinnaker for failing "to offer any way of narrowing the" requests lacks merit. Ms. Millare, not Spinnaker, has sought the entirety of the files of approximately 3,509 claims, none of which she has provided any reasonable basis to believe will provide probative information. Under these circumstances, if Ms. Millare believes narrowed requests are feasible and not unduly burdensome, she should initiate the discussion among counsel about how her requests could be narrowed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: SHONA L. RABARA VS. MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,220.00; Declaration Of Andre Karchemsky
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, March 20, 2025 Line 7. DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC.'s Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,220.00. Defendant Mercedes-Benz's motion to compel the deposition for plaintiff Shona Rabara and for monetary sanctions is granted as to the motion to compel and Ms. Rabara must pay reasonable monetary sanctions of $1,500 to Mercedes-Benz no later than April 18, 2025. If Ms. Rabara's deposition has not already occurred and has not been scheduled per the mutual agreement of the parties, Ms. Rabara's deposition must take place on April 18, 2025 or such other date as the parties mutually agree. Ms. Rabara's failure to timely provide available dates for her deposition lacked substantial justification. The requested amount of monetary sanctions is reduced to reflect the extreme simplicity of this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: URIEL SALAMANCA LOPEZ ET AL VS. ALL NATURAL STONE BURLINGAME, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, March 20, 2025 Line 3. 2 - DEFENDANT LOWE'S HOME CENTERS, LLC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Lowe's Home Centers' motion to strike punitive damages allegations and specified portions of the complaint filed by plaintiffs Uriel Lopez and Pamela Rodriguez is denied in its entirety. Plaintiffs adequately allege ultimate facts showing conscious disregard and despicable conduct malice and a duty to warn for fraudulent concealment which sufficiently support their prayer for punitive damages. (Nissan Motor Acceptance Cases (2021) 63 Cal.App.5th 793, 829 ["Of course, fraudulent concealment is an intentional tort that may support a punitive damage award"].) Lowe's has not shown that any portions of the complaint should be stricken. Lowe's request for judicial notice is denied. It is a longstanding and well-established rule that trial court opinions lack any precedential value and are not citable as legal authority in California courts. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: URIEL SALAMANCA LOPEZ ET AL VS. ALL NATURAL STONE BURLINGAME, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, March 20, 2025 Line 3. 1 - DEFENDANT LOWE'S HOME CENTERS, LLC DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant Lowe's Home Centers' demurrer to all six causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Uriel Lopez and Pamela Rodriguez is overruled as to all six causes of action. All six causes of action are adequately alleged and are not uncertain. Lowe's request for judicial notice is denied. It is a longstanding and well-established rule that trial court opinions lack any precedential value and are not citable as legal authority in California courts. Plaintiffs sue Lowe's for selling silica-containing stone products and alleges that Lowe's knew that there are toxic hazards associated with those products. Lowe's argues that plaintiffs did not allege a duty supporting their first cause of action for negligence because "according to the Complaint, Lowe's was not involved in the chain of commerce." The complaint alleges otherwise. The second and third causes of action for products liability sufficiently identify a defective product. The fourth cause of action for fraudulent concealment adequately alleges a duty to warn. (Jones v. ConocoPhillips Co. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1187, 1200.) The fifth cause of action for breach of implied warranties adequately alleges that the stone products were defective and that Lowe's impliedly warranted that they would be fit for their intended uses. Furthermore, Jones v. ConocoPhillips Co. controls here as well. There, the Court of Appeal held that a breach of implied warranty cause of action "is viable as to potentially hazardous chemical products manufactured and sold to companies for use by their own employees in the manufacturing process." (Id. at 1201-1202). The sixth cause of action for loss of consortium adequately alleges causation. A demurrer based on asserted uncertainty is disfavored and should only be granted when the complaint is so incomprehensible that it is impossible to respond. (Lickiss v. Financial Indus. Regulatory Auth. (2012) 208 Cal.App.5th 1125, 1135.) Plaintiffs' complaint easily passes this low bar. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PREFERRED BANK VS. ERIC K. CHUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT /Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment - Notice To Defendant Twiggy Tang
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, March 20, 2025 Line 4. 2 - PLAINTIFF PREFERRED BANK MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT Order For Issuance To Defendant Twiggy Tang. Plaintiff Preferred Bank's application for right to attach order and writ of attachment as to defendant Twiggy Tang is denied. A declaration supporting an attachment application must state facts with particularity and the declarant must show that he or she "can testify competently to the facts stated therein." (CCP 482.040.) The court sustains defendant Ms. Tang's objection to the declaration of Aaron Fan. (CCP 484.090(a)(2).) While Mr.Fan states that he is the Assistant Vice President of plaintiff and asserts that there were various breaches of the underlying loan by the borrower, he fails to establish a foundation for his assertions. Mr. Fan does not identify, much less attach, what, if any, records he relies upon for his assertions of breaches or whether those records satisfy the required elements for admissibility of business records. His failure to do so is in sharp contrast with the declarant in Lorber Industries v. Turbulence, Inc. (1985) 175 Cal. App, 3d 532, 536, a case that he relies on in his reply brief, who attached records that showed the key facts establishing entitlement to a right to attach order. Moreover, Mr. Fan does not even explain why Preferred Bak is entitled to the $7,769.92 in excess of the guaranty amount. (Royals v. Lu (2022) 81 Cal.App.5th 328, 350 [denying attachment where amount claimed uncertain].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)
